description: THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE (NPS) SEEKS TO COMPLETE AN ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT (EIS) TO ANALYZE A PROPOSAL AND A RANGE OF ALTERNATIVES FOR CONSTRUCTING A HIGH VOLTAGE ELECTRIC TRANSMISSION LINE AFFECTING FOUR NPS UNITS AND A NATIONAL FOREST IN MARYLAND, WEST VIRGINIA AND VIRGINIA. THESE FEDERAL LANDS INCLUDE CHESAPEAKE AND OHIO CANAL NATIONAL HISTORICAL PARK (CHOH), HARPERS FERRY NATIONAL HISTORICAL PARK (HAFE), APPALACHIAN NATIONAL SCENIC TRAIL (APPA), POTOMAC HERITAGE NATIONAL SCENIC TRAIL (PNST) AND MONONGAHELA NATIONAL FOREST (MNF) (SEE MAP ON PAGE 4). THE CONTRACTOR SHALL COMPLETE PHASE 1 OF A THREE PHASE PROJECT BY BEGINNING PREPARATION OF A DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT (EIS) FOR THE POTOMAC-APPALACHIAN TRANSMISSION HIGHLINE (PATH).  PHASE 1 SHALL INCLUDE (1) INTERNAL SCOPING, (2) PROJECT SCHEDULE AND STATUS CALLS, (3) ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, (4) TECHNICAL AND COMPLIANCE REVIEW OF PROPOSAL, (5) DATA COLLECTION FOR ENVIRONMENTAL ANALYSIS AND FOR DEVELOPING THE ALTERNATIVES, (6) PUBLIC SCOPING, AND (7) PUBLIC COMMENT ANALYSIS.  THE SECOND PHASE MAY BE AWARDED AS ADDITIONAL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE. THE SECOND PHASE INCLUDES PREPARATION AND FACILITATION OF DEVELOPMENT OF ALTERNATIVES, INCLUDING (1) ANY ADDITIONAL FIELD SURVEYS TO FILL DATA GAPS NOT CONDUCTED IN PHASE I, (2) CONCEPTUAL PLANS THAT DESCRIBE AND ILLUSTRATE THE ALTERNATIVES, (3) COST ESTIMATES FOR EACH ALTERNATIVE, (4) ALTERNATIVES MEETING PREPARATION, (5) PUBLIC ALTERNATIVES WORKSHOPS, (6) SUMMARY OF PUBLIC COMMENT ON ALTERNATIVES, (7) A VALUE ANALYSIS PROCESS FOR IDENTIFYING THE PREFERRED ALTERNATIVE, (8) CONTINUE TO MAINTAIN THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, AND (9) CONTINUE MONTHLY STATUS CALLS. OPTIONS FOR DEVELOPING THE ALTERNATIVES UNDER PHASE 2 ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THIS AWARD OF THE DELIVERY ORDER, BUT MAY BE ADDED VIA MODIFICATION TO THIS DELIVERY ORDER OR A SEPARATE DELIVERY ORDER AT A LATER DATE.  THE THIRD PHASE MAY ALSO BE AWARDED AS ADDITIONAL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE. THE THIRD PHASE INCLUDES COMPLETING THE EIS AND ASSOCIATED DOCUMENTS. UNDER THE THIRD PHASE THE CONTRACTOR WOULD COMPLETE: (1) TWO INTERNAL DRAFT EISS WITH  REVISIONS AND REPRINTING AFTER EACH REVIEW, (2) OTHER REQUIRED ENVIRONMENTAL AND REGULATORY DOCUMENTS, (3) A CAMERA READY DRAFT EIS FOR PUBLIC REVIEW, (4) PUBLIC REVIEW OF THE DRAFT EIS, (5) ANALYSIS OF ALL COMMENTS ON THE DRAFT EIS, (6) A FINAL EIS, (7) NOTICES OF AVAILABILITY, (8) A RECORD OF DECISION, (9) ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, AND (10) MONTHLY STATUS CALLS.  OPTIONS FOR COMPLETION OF THE EIS UNDER PHASE 3 ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THIS AWARD OF THE DELIVERY ORDER, BUT MAY BE ADDED VIA MODIFICATION TO THIS DELIVERY ORDER OR A SEPARATE DELIVERY ORDER AT A LATER DATE.

ENVIRONMENTAL ASSESSMENT

THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE (NPS) IS IN THE PROCESS OF PLANNING FOR REPAIRS AND STABILIZATION OF PORTIONS OF THE YORK RIVER SHORELINE THAT HAVE BEEN SEVERELY ERODING AND THREATENING THE STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY OF THE COLONIAL PARKWAY ROADWAY PRISM. SIGNIFICANT AREAS OF SHORELINE EROSION HAVE AFFECTED THE YORKTOWN BATTLEFIELD, IN ADDITION TO THE COLONIAL PARKWAY.   THE SCOPE OF WORK (SOW) HAS BEEN BROKEN OUT INTO THREE PARTS FOR THIS PARTICULAR PROJECT.  THE FIRST SOW DIRECTS THE CONTRACTOR TO PREPARE AN ENVIRONMENTAL ASSESSMENT (EA) TO ASSESS THE IMPACTS OF POTENTIAL ALTERNATIVES FOR THIS WORK.  THE WORK INCLUDES PREPARING THE APPROPRIATE DRAFT DECISION DOCUMENT (FINDING OF NO SIGNIFICANT IMPACT [FONSI] OR NOTICE OF INTENT [NOI]). THE SECOND SOW INCLUDES PREPARING A FEDERAL COASTAL CONSISTENCY DETERMINATION, PREPARING A WETLAND DELINEATION WITHIN THE AREA OF POTENTIAL EFFECT, AND THE THIRD SOW IS FOR PREPARING A WETLAND STATEMENT OF FINDINGS AND A FLOODPLAIN STATEMENT OF FINDINGS (SOF), DOCUMENTING COMPLIANCE WITH THE NPS DIRECTOR'S ORDER AND PROCEDURAL MANUAL #77-1 AND NPS EXECUTIVE ORDER 11988. THE WETLAND SOF AND THE FLOODPLAIN SOF MAY BE COMBINED INTO ONE WETLAND/FLOODPLAIN SOF, AS LONG AS THE REQUIREMENTS FOR BOTH DOCUMENTS ARE MET.  ALTHOUGH THERE ARE THREE SOWS, THE CONTRACTOR SHOULD SUBMIT ONLY ONE PRICE PROPOSAL.

THIS SCOPE OF SERVICES DIRECTS THE CONTRACTOR TO PREPARE AN ENVIRONMENTAL ASSESSMENT (EA) AND A GRAVEL MANAGEMENT PLAN FOR APPROXIMATELY SIX (6) CHOSEN GRAVEL MATERIAL SITES (BORROW PITS) LOCATED ALONG THE SALINE VALLEY ROAD ROAD AT DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK (HEREAFTER REFERRED TO AS THE PARK). THESE MATERIAL SITES ARE PROPOSED FOR USE BY INYO COUNTY BUT ARE WITHIN THE BOUNDARIES OF DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK.  THE PARK WILL DESIGNATE WHICH BORROW SITES WILL BE STUDIED FURTHER UNDER THIS SCOPE OF SERVICES DOCUMENT. THE GRAVEL MANAGEMENT PLAN WILL BE DEVELOPED AS A SEPARATE DOCUMENT THAT INCORPORATES THE FINDINGS OF THE EA BY REFERENCE. IF THE PARK DETERMINES THAT THE PROPOSED ACTION ALTERNATIVE MAY AFFECT FEDERALLY LISTED SPECIES, THE CONTRACTOR WOULD BE RESPONSIBLE FOR PREPARATION OF A BIOLOGICAL ASSESSMENT AND FULFILLMENT OF SECTION 7 CONSULTATION REQUIREMENTS.  CONSULTATION WOULD BE COMPLETED PRIOR TO COMPLETION OF THE FINAL FONSI.  THE WORK GENERALLY CONSISTS OF PREPARING A DRAFT EA AND DRAFT GRAVEL MANAGEMENT PLAN FOR THREE (3) INTERNAL NATIONAL PARK SERVICE (NPS) REVIEWS; MAKING APPROPRIATE REVISIONS TO THE EA AND IN RESPONSE TO NPS REVIEW COMMENTS; PREPARING AND SUBMITTING THE FINAL EA (WITH THE PROPOSED GRAVEL MANAGEMENT PLAN INCLUDED AS AN APPENDIX) DOCUMENT; PRINTING AND DISTRIBUTING INTERNAL DRAFTS,  THE FINAL PUBLIC REVIEW DRAFT EA, AND FINAL GRAVEL MANAGEMENT PLAN; AND PREPARING THE APPROPRIATE DECISION DOCUMENT (FINDING OF NO SIGNIFICANT IMPACT [FONSI] OR NOTICE OF INTENT [NOI]). IT IS ANTICIPATED THIS EFFORT WILL RESULT IN A FONSI. IF AN NOI IS NEEDED, A SAMPLE WILL BE PROVIDED.
